items.back: Inform aircraft when approaching glidepath, approximately 10 to 30 seconds before final descent. Phraseology: 'APPROACHING GLIDEPATH.'

Discontinue PAR. Tell aircraft: take over visually or execute missed approach. If surveillance approach available for same runway, offer it. Using PAR azimuth for surveillance: mileages from touchdown. For PAR azimuth without glideslope runways, inform pilot PAR azimuth will be used.

PAR approaches may be conducted when ASR is unusable provided: nonradar approach positions aircraft over navaid/DME fix within PAR coverage, OR adjacent radar facility can provide direct radar handoff to PAR controller.
Provide DH to any pilot who requests it. Phraseology: 'DECISION HEIGHT (number of feet).'
When aircraft reaches the point where final descent is to start, instruct: 'BEGIN DESCENT.'

Transmissions every ~5 seconds. Issue: HEADING, ON GLIDEPATH, ON COURSE, SLIGHTLY/WELL ABOVE/BELOW GLIDEPATH, SLIGHTLY/WELL LEFT/RIGHT OF COURSE. Trend info with RAPIDLY/SLOWLY modifiers. Do not key transmitter continuously.
Inform aircraft of distance from touchdown at least once each mile on final approach. Phraseology: '(Number of miles) MILES FROM TOUCHDOWN.'
Inform the aircraft when it reaches the published decision height. Phraseology: 'AT DECISION HEIGHT.'
(a) Continue glidepath/course info until aircraft passes over threshold (advisory only below DH). (b) 'OVER APPROACH LIGHTS.' (c) 'OVER LANDING THRESHOLD, (position relative to course).'

MUST discontinue: (1) pilot requests, (2) safe approach to threshold questionable, (3) over landing threshold, (4) pilot reports lights AND requests/advises visual. Note: 'runway in sight' alone is NOT a request to proceed visually. After visual: all PAR procedures discontinued. Monitor final and frequency until touchdown.

Issue transfer slightly delayed until aircraft on landing roll-out (to avoid diverting pilot attention during transition/touchdown). Phraseology: 'CONTACT (terminal control function) (frequency, if required) AFTER LANDING.'
